To the Editor of The Press: l
have discussed publicly, the clause
in the new city charter that per-
mits ordinances to be passed upon
on emergency without pending one
hour, and at the same time, author
iZing the expenditure of public
money. thus rendering it ridiculous
to claim that there be such a thing
as referendum in such grant of I'M
limited power. But the charter
further provides, with reference to
local improvements:


IF the owners of one-half of the
property subject to assessment for
any local improvement, file a petl-
ton with the council requesting
that local improvement be made,
the council, by majority vote may
order such improvement. Without
usch petition four-fifths vote of
the whole council. shall be neceS-
sary to order such improvement,
and further. any irregularity in the
petition for an improvement is i'm
material where the ordinance Or
dering the improvement is passed
by a four-fifths vote of the council


There never was such power un-
der a republican form of govern
ment. state or municipal, lodged ar
bitrarily and finally, without re-
view, in the hands of three men, a
majority of the commission of five.
A peril lies at the door of every
laboring man who either owns, or
is buying home; threatens the
safety of investment in the city,
and makes it dangerous to invest.
Any morning an owner of property
may see contractors outfit build
ing a sewer or paving a street in
front of his property, at the mere
will of four commissioners, and
without any petition from the prop
erty owner. The work can be let.
without competition, to fake pat
ented paving concern, or to any
contractor, and at a price that Is
fixed by the commission and the
contractor. and without competf-
ton. The property owner must foot
the bills, although he has not been
consulted. Is there any laboring
